---
title: "Project Hail Mary"
subtitle: "DNAzyme-like motifs, horizontal gene transfer, and short linear motif conservation in Potyviridae"
author:
  - name: "Christina Muedsam"
    affiliation: "currently none"
date: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Preprint / work in progress — not peer reviewed
This compendium is **under active development and has not been peer reviewed**.
Much of it is **exploratory**, and several of the central ideas (in particular the
DNAzyme-activity, horizontal-gene-transfer, and AP-4 / tyrosine-motif immune
hypotheses) are presented as **explicit, testable hypotheses rather than
established results** — they are flagged as such throughout. Analyses, figures,
interpretations and conclusions may change; please treat everything here as a
living draft and cite with that caveat.
:::

## Overview

This compendium documents the discovery and characterisation of a DNAzyme-like
catalytic domain sequence that maps precisely to the genomes of multiple
Potyviridae species, with flanking regions matching eukaryotic genes across
plant, fungal, and animal kingdoms. Cross-kingdom horizontal gene transfer is
the most parsimonious explanation for this pattern.

The work spans several interconnected research threads:

| Chapter | Topic | Key tools |
|---|---|---|
| [1 — Discovery](notebooks/01_discovery.qmd) | DNAzyme match + eukaryotic homologs | BLAST, Biostrings |
| [2 — Phylogenetics](notebooks/02_phylogenetics.qmd) | CI protein tree, motif mapping | MAFFT, IQ-TREE 2, ape |
| [3 — Codon Usage](notebooks/03_codon_dnazyme.qmd) | RSCU, ENC, host adaptation, DNAzyme locus | potytools, seqinr |
| [4 — ELM Motifs](notebooks/04_protein_motifs.qmd) | Short linear motif conservation in CI | ELM REST API, potytools |
| [5 — HGT](notebooks/05_hgt.qmd) | Phylogenetic incongruence, sequence logos | ggseqlogo, potytools |
| [5b — Networks](notebooks/05b_networks.qmd) | Host-virus ecological networks | igraph, BioNexus app |
| [6 — Immunity](notebooks/06_immu_simmu.qmd) | PTI/ETI simulation under genetic perturbations | Observable JS |

## Reproducibility

All computationally intensive steps (alignment, phylogenetic inference, NCBI
fetching, ELM queries) are orchestrated by a `targets` pipeline:

```r
# Install the pipeline dependencies once
install.packages(c("targets", "tarchetypes"))
remotes::install_local("packages/potytools")

# Run the full pipeline (skips up-to-date steps automatically)
targets::tar_make()

# Visualise dependencies
targets::tar_visnetwork()
```

External tools required on `PATH` for running the entire pipeline: `mafft`, `iqtree2`, `hmmscan`.

## Interactive Companion App

The **BioNexus** Shiny app provides live GBIF + GLOBI biodiversity network
exploration relevant to Chapters 5b and 6:

```r
shiny::runApp("shiny/bionexus/")
```

## Publication Plans

This material is intended for at least two separate publications:

1. **Paper 1** — The DNAzyme discovery + HGT evidence (Chapters 1, 2, 5).
   Target: *PNAS* / *eLife* / *PLoS Pathogens*.
2. **Paper 2** — ELM short linear motif conservation in Potyviridae CI proteins
   (Chapters 3, 4). Target: *Virus Evolution* / *Journal of Virology*.
3. **Paper 3** (optional) — Ecological host networks and immunity modelling
   (Chapters 5b, 6). Target: *New Phytologist* / *MPMI*.

## Package

Analysis functions are collected in the **potytools** R package
(`packages/potytools/`), being prepared for rOpenSci peer review.

## Project History

| Date | Milestone |
|---|---|
| Feb 2026 | DNAzyme match first identified (`2025_toys/poty.qmd`) |
| Mar 2026 | HGT analysis suite (`2025_toys/poty3-hgt-claude.Rmd`) |
| Apr 2026 | Codon usage analysis iterations (`2026_claude/`, `2026_PotyTest/`) |
| May 2026 | Cleaned codon + DNAzyme notebook (`2026_cleanPoty/`) |
| May 2026 | Final phylogenetic pipeline (`2026_CopilotPoty/`) |
| Jun 2026 | ELM motif & immunity notebooks; this compendium |
